articles.title: Eight girls arrested on suspicion of arson after Kenya school fire
articles.summary: The fire broke out at the Utumishi Girls Academy Senior School in Gilgil, killing 16 people and wounding 79 others.
articles.titleZh: 肯尼亚学校火灾后八名女孩因涉嫌纵火被捕
articles.summaryZh: 吉尔吉尔乌图米希女子学院高中发生火灾，造成 16 人死亡、79 人受伤。

contentParagraphs.en: The fire broke out at the Utumishi Girls Academy Senior School in Gilgil, killing 16 people and wounding 79 others.At least eight students have been arrested on suspicion of arson after fire at a boarding school for girls in Kenya killed 16 students and injured 79, police said.The fire broke out in the early hours of Thursday at the Utumishi Girls Academy Senior School in Gilgil, west-central Kenya.Recommended Stories list of 3 itemslist 1 of 3At least 16 students killed in fire at Kenya girls school dormlist 2 of 3Four reported killed, 30 injured, in Kenyan fuel price protestslist 3 of 3Kenyan court suspends US Ebola quarantine facility planend of listOn Friday, the Directorate of Criminal Investigation said preliminary investigations had identified eight people as “persons of interest in connection with the planning and execution of the suspected arson attack”.“The eight girls have since been arrested and are currently in police custody,” the statement added.Reporting from the school, Al Jazeera’s Catherine Soi said police are still investigating potential arson.“We have been speaking to parents who have been here since the early morning, they were very frustrated earlier because nobody was giving them information,” she said.“We have parents who say they haven’t seen their children at all – I guess those are the ones, the students [who] died – and then we have parents of whose loved ones, the students, are still inside being interrogated,” Soi added.Relatives wait for news at the Utumishi Girls Academy in Gilgil, following Thursday’s fire, May 28, 2026 [AFP]Student Hilda Njeri, who was in one of the dorms most-affected by the fire, told Al Jazeera she was still dealing with everything that happened.“I was badly injured on my leg, and my lower back was badly injured,” Njeri said outside the school on Friday, adding that the principal took the students to hospital and paid all bills for treatment.“The fire was very [big]; we could not pass through the fire because we had no water to put out the fire, so we had to jump through the window,” she said, adding that she struggled to breathe while inside the building.Kenyan Education Minister Julius Ogamba told reporters that early investigations found that two teachers had been informed of the students’ alleged plans, but failed to stop them.Ogamba added that the school failed to follow safety rules, citing overcrowding in the dorms and a locked emergency exit.The Kenyan government has disbanded the school board of management and will take appropriate legal and disciplinary action against any staff found to have neglected their duties, he said.Thursday’s school fire follows others in Kenya. In 2024, a blaze in a primary boarding school in Nyeri County killed 21 students. In 2001, 67 schoolboys at Kyanguli Secondary School were killed in an arson attack.
contentParagraphs.zh: 吉尔吉尔乌图米什女子学院高中发生火灾，造成 16 人死亡、79 人受伤。警方称，肯尼亚一所女子寄宿学校发生火灾，造成 16 名学生死亡、79 人受伤，至少 8 名学生因涉嫌纵火被捕。肯尼亚中西部吉尔吉尔的乌图米什女子学院高中于周四凌晨发生火灾。推荐故事列表 3 条中的 1 条至少 16 条学生在肯尼亚女子学校宿舍火灾中丧生 名单中的 2 名学生在肯尼亚燃油价格抗议活动中丧生，30 人受伤 名单中的 3 名 肯尼亚法院暂停美国埃博拉检疫设施计划 名单周五，刑事调查局表示，初步调查已确定八人为“与策划和执行涉嫌纵火袭击有关的人员”。“这八名女孩已被捕，目前被警方拘留，”声明补充说。来自学校的报道，半岛电视台的凯瑟琳·索伊表示，警方仍在调查潜在的纵火案。“我们一直在与从一大早就到这里的家长交谈，他们早些时候非常沮丧，因为没有人向他们提供信息，”她说。“我们有家长说他们根本没有见过自己的孩子——我猜就是那些死的学生——然后我们还有他们的亲人、学生的父母仍在里面接受审问，”索伊2026 年 5 月 28 日星期四发生火灾后，亲戚们在吉尔吉尔的乌图米希女子学院等待消息。 [法新社]学生希尔达·恩杰里 (Hilda Njeri) 住在受火灾影响最严重的宿舍之一，她告诉半岛电视台，她仍在处理所发生的一切。“我的腿受了重伤，我的下背部也受了重伤，”恩杰里周五在学校外说，并补充说校长她说：“火势非常大，我们无法通过火场，因为我们没有水来灭火，所以我们不得不从窗户跳进去。”她补充说，她在大楼内时呼吸困难。肯尼亚教育部长朱利叶斯·奥甘巴告诉记者，早期调查发现两名教师已被告知学生们所谓的计划，但未能阻止他们。奥甘巴补充说，学校没有遵守安全规则，理由是宿舍过度拥挤，紧急出口上锁。他说，肯尼亚政府已经解散了学校管理委员会，并将对任何被发现玩忽职守的员工采取适当的法律和纪律行动。周四，肯尼亚的其他学校也发生火灾。 2024年，涅里县一所小学寄宿学校发生火灾，导致21名学生死亡。 2001年，Kyanguli中学的67名男生在一次纵火袭击中丧生。

articles.title: Why I’m grateful to the Pope for his encyclical on AI | Francine Prose
articles.summary:
The intelligent and thoughtful encyclical is an important warning of the uses and misuses of a rapidly developing technology. Silicon Valley is wrong to dismiss it
Often I’m asked if I think that the novels of the future will all be written by AI. It’s not so much a question as a
articles.titleZh: 为什么我感谢教皇关于人工智能的通谕 |弗朗辛·散文
articles.summaryZh:
这份明智而深思熟虑的通谕是对快速发展的技术的使用和滥用的重要警告。硅谷忽视它是错误的
经常有人问我是否认为未来的小说都会由人工智能来写。这与其说是一个问题，不如说是一个

contentParagraphs.en: Often I’m asked if I think that the novels of the future will all be written by AI. It’s not so much a question as a provocation. Do I worry that a machine can do what I do, only better? I usually say something like: “No algorithm is going to write Anna Karenina!” which is also not a real answer.So I’m grateful to Pope Leo XIV, the American pope, for his recently issued letter to the world, Magnifica Humanitas: On Safeguarding the Human Person in the Time of Artificial Intelligence. It’s a long (more than 40,00 words), intelligent and thoughtful encyclical in which the pope addresses the uses and misuses of a rapidly developing technology. Now when someone asks my opinion of AI, I can refer them to the pope’s letter, or at least chapter three.The encyclical begins with an appropriately biblical reference to the tragic consequences of a breakdown in human communication. Humanity faces a “pivotal choice: either to construct a new Tower of Babel or to build a city in which God and humanity dwell together”. What follows is a detailed account of the evolution of the views of Pope Leo’s predecessors, of the Vatican’s ideas about labor, authority, government, science, power and our moral obligation to one another. It cites the work that the church has done in defense of human dignity and freedom.The third chapter, Technology and Dominance. The Grandeur of Humanity in Light of the Promises of AI, delivers on the promise of the encyclical’s title. In an eloquent (and most often quoted) passage explaining what AI is not, the pope essentially defines what it means to be human. “So-called artificial intelligences do not undergo experiences, do not possess a body, do not feel joy or pain, do not mature through relationships and do not know from within what love, work, friendship or responsibility mean.” AI does not have a moral conscience nor does it show any guiding concern for the greater human good.The letter proceeds to say the most important and necessary things about what is possibly the greatest threat posed by AI: it can be programmed solely to maximize profit, a situation that can only result in the suffering of the many for the benefit of the few. The pope warns against the “manipulation of privacy” and the “misuse of information”, against the uses of an algorithm to manage employment, to control access to public services and credit, and to elevate or damage one’s personal reputation.Compassion, mercy and forgiveness – not high on the machine’s list of priorities – will become obsolete. “‘Necessary sacrifices’ may begin to be justified, placing the burden on the most vulnerable in pursuit of the supposed optimization of the species.” If the tools of this new power are placed in the hands of those who already possess wealth and influence, they will be used to elevate the comfort, health and wellbeing of our wealthiest and most privileged citizens.As the letter nears its end, the pope calls on us to remain faithful to the truth, to invest in education, to cultivate relationships, to live in justice and peace – to resist the way in which the new technologies can “exploit the most vulnerable, create new forms of slavery and derive profit from conflict”. What becomes clear is that the pope is not condemning AI outright but rather the way it can be used as a tool of political repression and as a guarantee of worsening economic inequality.In theory, it’s possible to criticize the encyclical for not going far enough, for not using another biblical metaphor – the golden calf – to stigmatize the use of AI because of how it prioritizes cost-saving over spiritual, individual and communal growth. But that ship has already sailed, and there’s not much that Leo XIV – or any religious leader – can do to condemn the new advances as a 21st-century form of idolatry.Even so, the encyclical’s vision of human nature, of the spirit of justice and empathy that needs to prevail, of the essential importance of the highest moral values – is ultimately so beneficent, so positive, so generous, so inarguably clear about our obligation to protect the weak and the poor that it’s hard to find reasons to dismiss it.But that’s the scary part. Apparently there’s been a certain amount of blowback from Silicon Valley, where the inventors and masters of the latest technology have suggested that the pope doesn’t know what he’s talking about.Jeremy Nixon, a founder of AGI House, a group dedicated to proving that AI is essentially equal to the human brain, was quoted in the New York Times as saying that the church hadn’t “thought deeply about … AI”, adding: “They couldn’t have a position on it, because they don’t understand it.” And there seems to be a widespread belief that the end product of the current research will be, in effect a new God, or at least a convincing simulacrum. Concerned about the perils of the future, our society is choosing to overlook the evidence that the downsides of AI are already upon us. A friend’s daughter, a college student majoring in advertising, was recently informed by her adviser that by the time she graduates, all the jobs in advertising will have been taken by AI.If the masters of this new technology fail to agree with what the pope sees as its dangers and drawbacks, we are in very deep trouble indeed. The problem is not that we will have a robot writing Anna Karenina. The problem is that no one will see any possible need for a novel that so exquisitely portrays the sufferings of a woman, a singular human being.There would be no point in a book like that unless that account of a life-changing mistake could be monetized by a forward-thinking tech bro and used to finance the purchase of a bigger and better yacht, presumably serviced by a permanent underclass, by workers whose dignity – whose formerly valued and valuable jobs – have been pirated by the rapacious manifestations of artificial intelligence.
contentParagraphs.zh: 经常有人问我是否认为未来的小说都会由人工智能来写。与其说这是一个问题，不如说是一种挑衅。我是否担心机器可以做我所做的事情，甚至做得更好？我通常会这样说：“没有算法能够写出安娜·卡列尼娜！”这也不是一个真正的答案。所以我感谢美国教皇利奥十四世最近向世界发出了一封信，《人性的伟大：论人工智能时代保护人类》。这是一部长篇（超过 40,00 字）、智慧且深思熟虑的通谕，其中教皇谈到了快速发展的技术的使用和误用。现在，当有人问我对人工智能的看法时，我可以向他们推荐教皇的信，或者至少是第三章。通谕首先恰当地引用了圣经，提到了人类交流中断的悲惨后果。人类面临着“关键的选择：要么建造一座新的巴别塔，要么建造一座上帝与人类共同居住的城市”。以下详细介绍了利奥教皇的前任观点的演变，以及梵蒂冈关于劳动、权威、政府、科学、权力以及我们彼此之间的道德义务的观念的演变。它列举了教会在捍卫人类尊严和自由方面所做的工作。第三章，技术与统治。 《人工智能的承诺下的人类的伟大》兑现了通谕标题的承诺。在一段雄辩的（也是最常被引用的）解释人工智能不是什么的段落中，教皇本质上定义了人类的意义。 “所谓的人工智能不会经历经历，不会拥有身体，不会感受快乐或痛苦，不会通过人际关系而成熟，也不知道内心意味着什么爱、工作、友谊或责任。”人工智能没有道德良知，也没有对人类更大的利益表现出任何指导性的关注。这封信接着谈到了人工智能可能构成的最大威胁的最重要和最必要的事情：它只能被编程以实现利润最大化，这种情况只会导致大多数人的痛苦，而少数人的利益。教皇警告不要“操纵隐私”和“滥用信息”，不要使用算法来管理就业、控制公共服务和信贷的获取，以及提升或损害个人声誉。同情、仁慈和宽恕——在机器的优先事项清单上并不靠前——将变得过时。 “‘必要的牺牲’可能开始变得合理，为追求所谓的物种优化而将负担强加给最脆弱的群体。”如果这种新权力的工具被置于那些已经拥有财富和影响力的人手中，它们将被用来提高我们最富有和最有特权的公民的舒适、健康和福祉。在这封信即将结束之际，教宗呼吁我们继续忠于真理，投资于教育，培养关系，生活在正义与和平中，抵制新技术“剥削最弱势群体，创造新形式的奴隶制并从冲突中获利”的方式。显而易见的是，教皇并不是直接谴责人工智能，而是谴责它被用作政治镇压的工具和加剧经济不平等的保证。从理论上讲，我们可以批评通谕走得不够远，没有使用另一个圣经隐喻——金牛犊——来污蔑人工智能的使用，因为它优先考虑节省成本而不是精神、个人和社区的增长。但这艘船已经起航了，利奥十四世——或者任何宗教领袖——都无法谴责这些新进步是21世纪的偶像崇拜。即便如此，通谕对人性、需要占上风的正义和同理心精神、最高道德价值观的至关重要性的愿景，最终还是如此仁慈、如此积极、如此慷慨，如此无可争议地明确了我们保护弱者和穷人的义务，很难找到拒绝它的理由。但这就是可怕的部分。显然，硅谷已经产生了一定程度的反弹，那里的最新技术的发明者和大师表示，教皇不知道他在说什么。 《纽约时报》援引 AGI House 创始人杰里米·尼克松 (Jeremy Nixon) 的话说，教会没有“深入思考……人工智能”，并补充道：“他们不可能对此有立场，因为他们不了解它。”人们似乎普遍相信，当前研究的最终产品实际上将是一个新的上帝，或者至少是一个令人信服的拟像。出于对未来危险的担忧，我们的社会选择忽视人工智能的弊端已经降临到我们身上的证据。一位朋友的女儿是一名广告专业的大学生，最近她的导师告诉她，到她毕业时，广告行业的所有工作都将被人工智能取代。如果这项新技术的大师们不同意教皇所认为的其危险和缺点，我们确实陷入了很大的麻烦。问题不在于我们将有一个机器人来写《安娜·卡列尼娜》。问题是，没有人会认为有必要写一部如此精美地描绘一个女人、一个独特的人类的痛苦的小说。除非对一个改变人生的错误的描述能够被一位有远见的技术兄弟货币化，并用它来资助购买一艘更大更好的游艇，而这艘游艇的服务人员大概是永久的下层阶级，而这些工人的尊严——他们以前有价值和有价值的工作——已经被贪婪的人工智能表现所盗版，那么这样的书就没有意义了。情报。

articles.title: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans
articles.summary: Explosion of Blue Origin rocket is a setback for the company and for Nasa's Moon plans.

contentParagraphs.en: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans5 hours agoPallab GhoshScience correspondentThe fireball that lit up the sky over Florida's Kennedy Space Centre last night has put a big question mark over whether Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin can deliver on a string of commitments to Nasa in its efforts to send astronauts to the lunar surface and build a Moon base.Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded at about 21:00 local time during a routine test of its engines.The 98m (322ft) rocket had been due to launch 48 satellites for Amazon's Leo broadband network, as early as 4 June.The explosion is obviously a big setback for the Leo network, which is struggling to be the main competitor for Elon Musk's SpaceX and its Starlink service. But the ramifications will go much further.The good news was that no-one was injured, despite the spectacular explosion."All personnel are accounted for and safe," Bezos wrote on X. "Very rough day, but we'll rebuild whatever needs rebuilding and get back to flying. It's worth it."But the blast which tore through Space Launch Complex 36 (LC-36) has caused extensive damage. Footage shows one of the pad's lightning protection towers toppling in the aftermath.LC-36 is the only facility in the world built to launch the New Glenn rocket. That means that until the launch pad is rebuilt and re-certified, Blue Origin has no way to fly its largest rocket - and analysts expect that to take months, not weeks.The setback comes just days after Nasa's administrator, Jared Isaacman, announced the first three missions of the agency's plans to build a lunar base - a project he billed as the start of a "permanent presence" at the Moon's south pole.The first, Moon Base 1, is due to be flown on Blue Origin's robotic Blue Moon Mark 1 "Endurance" lander, and is targeted for launch no earlier than autumn 2026.It is intended to carry two Nasa science payloads to the Shackleton Connecting Ridge and demonstrate the precision-landing techniques needed to keep future crewed landings safe. But the lander was to ride to the Moon on top of a New Glenn - the same type of rocket that is now scattered across LC-36 - raising immediate doubts as to whether that timetable is now possible.Earlier this week, Nasa also handed Blue Origin a contract worth up to $468m to deliver two commercial lunar terrain vehicles, built by Astrolab and Lunar Outpost, to the Moon's south pole by 2028.Those rovers are meant to be in place before astronauts arrive. Nasa has set a target date of 2028 for a crewed landing, though that date had been questioned even before last night's explosion.Blue OriginThe explosion has cast doubts over Blue Origin's next launch timetableThe destroyed rocket had been due to deploy a batch of 48 satellites for Amazon's Leo broadband constellation - the network formerly known as Project Kuiper which is designed to challenge Elon Musk's Starlink.Just over 300 Amazon Leo satellites are currently in orbit, all of them lifted by SpaceX, United Launch Alliance and Arianespace rather than by Blue Origin itself.The gap between Leo and Starlink - which has more than 10,000 satellites in orbit - is now a serious commercial problem for Bezos' group. Under its US Federal Communications Commission (FCC) licence, Amazon is required to have half of its 3,236-satellite constellation in orbit by 30 July 2026. As of late May, the company was already more than 1,300 satellites short of that target, with delays blamed in part on "launch vehicle availability" from Blue Origin and other providers.With New Glenn now expected to be grounded for months, Amazon will be even more dependent on its rivals - chief among them SpaceX - to keep its rollout alive, and is almost certain to need a fresh extension to its timetable from the FCC.Elon Musk, SpaceX CEO, responded on X to footage of the blast saying only: "Most unfortunate. Rockets are hard."More problemsNASAEarlier this year, NASA launched the Artemis II mission with four astronauts aboard the Orion spacecraftNasa's next crewed Moon mission, Artemis III, is scheduled to launch next year and is designed to be a low earth orbit flight test of two commercial lunar landers - built by Blue Origin and SpaceX.Until the explosion, Blue Origin was seen as the more prepared of the two. Its Mark 1 demonstrator was already in final stacking in Florida, while SpaceX's Starship has yet to complete a successful in-space propellant transfer.All this leaves Nasa's plan to land astronauts back to the Moon by 2028 and to build a Moon base there with several problems which will inevitably now lead to delays. The lander test for Artemis III depends on the same rocket family, and the Moon Base rover deliveries are contractually tied to New Glenn.Meanwhile, China is forging ahead with its own plans to land its astronauts on the Moon by 2030, leaving Nasa without much room for manoeuvre.Nasa Administrator Jared Isaacman responded to the latest setback on X: "Spaceflight is unforgiving, and developing new heavy-lift launch capability is extraordinarily difficult."But Isaacman's drive to push Nasa's Moon programme to a more ambitious frequency of launches is now seriously in doubt after last night's setback.

articles.title: Kambo: What is the frog poison ‘spiritual ritual’ killing detoxers?
articles.summary: Indigenous groups have warned against the use of kambo by non-specialist practitioners.
articles.titleZh: Kambo：什么是青蛙毒“精神仪式”杀死解毒者？
articles.summaryZh: 土著团体警告非专业从业者不要使用坎博。

contentParagraphs.en: A number of people around the world have died after taking part in an ancient healing practice known as kambo, which involves ingesting frog poison.The spiritual ritual of kambo, which is promoted among Western “wellness communities” as a one‑stop cure for a spectrum of physical and mental ailments, from anxiety to arthritis, is not backed by any scientific evidence.In April this year, 40-year-old United Kingdom wellness coach and cancer survivor Kristian Trend was the latest to die after reportedly taking part in a “cleansing ceremony” which involved kambo, local media reported.Trend’s mother told the UK national newspaper, The Telegraph, that she wanted the practice to be banned. While it is legal to buy frog poison for kambo in the UK, it is not considered a licensed medicine.The practice has also been banned by other countries.What is kambo?Kambo is an ancient shamanic healing ritual with its roots in traditional South American medicine. It was practised by Amazonian Indigenous tribes centuries ago. The term “kambo” refers to the secretions of the giant monkey frog (Phyllomedusa bicolor), which are applied to small burns on the skin during the ritual.Experts say it is hard to pin down the exact origins of kambo since knowledge of the ritual was generally passed down orally, rather than being written down.The earliest known written documentation of the practice was made in 1925, when French missionary Constant Tastevin wrote about kambo being practised in the border regions between Peru and Brazil.The monkey frog actually secretes kambo to kill or harm predators that try to attack it. The secretions are also called sapo, which translates to “toad” in Spanish.Many amphibians release such toxins. In February this year, European countries accused Russia of using a toxin made from the secretions of South American dart frogs to kill Kremlin critic Alexey Navalny.How does kambo work?Kambo is an ancient spiritual and healing practice traditionally carried out by a specialised practitioner called a shaman.The frog’s secretions are collected and dried before the animal is released back into the wild. A heated rod is then used to make a series of small, superficial burns on the skin, and the dried secretion is applied to these points by the shaman, who recites hymns while waiting for the kambo to take effect.A bucket is usually placed in front of the person receiving the treatment in anticipation of vomiting – a common reaction to the ritual. Then, the kambo is wiped off the burns.Frog poison is dabbed onto superficial burns on the skin in the kambo ritual [Shutterstock]What is the kambo poison?Kambo is considered poisonous because it contains a complex mix of bioactive peptides – short chains of amino acids – including some that violently stimulate the digestive tract, causing intense nausea and vomiting, and others that act directly on the cardiovascular system, leading to rapid drops in blood pressure and fainting.Some peptides have opioid‑like effects, altering breathing patterns and affecting the central nervous system. In extreme cases, this combination can trigger sudden cardiac problems or severe organ toxicity, which can be fatal.Before the ritual, the person receiving kambo is often advised to drink large amounts of water. Combined with heavy vomiting and fluid shifts, this can lead to hyponatremia – dangerously low sodium levels in the blood, sometimes referred to as water intoxication. Severe hyponatremia can cause brain swelling, seizures, coma and, in the worst cases, brain death.Kambo also causes the gastrointestinal tract to contract violently, which can cause the oesophagus to rupture, also resulting in death.What is the kambo ritual for?Kambo is known as a “cleansing” ritual, believed to detox the body and, in some traditions, to enhance fertility, virility and even bring good luck.It is also used to treat diseases and disorders including addiction, Alzheimer’s disease, anxiety, cancer, chronic pain, depression, diabetes, hepatitis, HIV and AIDS, infections, rheumatism and vascular conditions, according to the US health information website, Healthline.But doctors say there is no scientific evidence that kambo is effective at treating any of these conditions, and modern medical case reports have instead linked kambo to severe illness and death.Healthline lists the possible side effects of kambo as nausea, vomiting, diarrhoea, abdominal pain, dizziness, heart palpitations, a sensation of a lump in the throat, difficulty swallowing, swelling of the lips, eyelids or face and even loss of bladder control.A man prepares frog poison for a kambo ceremony [Shutterstock]Who has been using it?Over the past two decades, kambo has become popular in Western and Latin American countries. It has been adopted in “wellness” circles and alternative medicine communities in Brazil, Australia, the UK and the United States.Indigenous experts have warned against the use of kambo by non-Indigenous practitioners, however. UK media recently reported that Yamanawa leader Joaquim Luz has criticised the online sale of kambo and warned that using it without the preparation or permission of Indigenous communities puts people at risk. The Yamanawa people originate in the Brazilian Amazon state of Acre, with communities extending into Peru and Bolivia.Who has died from kambo?There have been at least six known deaths associated with the use of kambo worldwide in recent years.In 2008, a 52-year-old man died in Brazil after receiving the kambo treatment.In 2018, a man in Italy died from an abnormal heart rate while experiencing kambo. The man had obesity and ventricular hypertrophy, a condition which causes the heart’s main pumping chambers to thicken.In 2019, Australian woman Natasha Lechner, 39, died while taking part in a kambo ceremony to cure her chronic back pain.In October 2021, Australian man Jarrad Antonovich died after kambo was performed on him. It was suspected that he died from a perforated oesophagu
contentParagraphs.zh: 世界各地有很多人在参加一种名为“kambo”的古老疗法后死亡，这种疗法涉及服用青蛙毒药。在西方“健康社区”中推广的“kambo”精神仪式是治疗从焦虑到关节炎等一系列身心疾病的一站式疗法，但这种仪式并没有任何科学证据支持。今年 4 月，40 岁的英国健康教练兼癌症幸存者克里斯蒂安·特伦德 (Kristian Trend) 是最新一位因参加一项名为“kambo”的疗法而去世的人。据当地媒体报道，涉及坎博的“净化仪式”。特伦德的母亲告诉英国全国性报纸《电讯报》，她希望禁止这种做法。虽然在英国购买青蛙毒药坎博是合法的，但它不被视为获得许可的药物。这种做法也被其他国家禁止。什么是坎博？坎博是一种古老的萨满治疗仪式，根源于传统南美医学。几个世纪前，亚马逊土著部落就已经实行了这种做法。 “kambo”一词指的是巨型猴蛙（Phyllomedusa bicolor）的分泌物，在仪式过程中将其涂抹在皮肤上的小烧伤处。专家表示，很难确定 kambo 的确切起源，因为有关仪式的知识通常是口头传承的，而不是书面记录。有关该做法的最早的书面记录是在 1925 年制作的，当时法国传教士 Constant Tastevin 写到，秘鲁和秘鲁之间的边境地区正在练习 kambo。巴西。猴蛙实际上会分泌坎博来杀死或伤害试图攻击它的掠食者。这种分泌物也被称为“sapo”，在西班牙语中翻译为“蟾蜍”。许多两栖动物都会释放此类毒素。今年2月，欧洲国家指责俄罗斯使用南美箭蛙分泌物制成的毒素杀死克里姆林宫批评家阿列克谢·纳瓦尔尼。卡姆博是如何发挥作用的？卡姆博是一种古老的精神和治疗实践，传统上由称为萨满的专业从业者进行。青蛙的分泌物被收集并干燥，然后将其放回野外。然后用一根加热的棒在皮肤上造成一系列小而浅的烧伤，萨满将干燥的分泌物涂抹在这些点上，萨满在等待 kambo 生效的同时背诵赞美诗。通常会在接受治疗的人面前放置一个水桶，以防呕吐——这是仪式的常见反应。然后，将 kambo 擦去烧伤处。在 kambo 仪式中，将青蛙毒药涂抹在皮肤浅表烧伤处 [Shutterstock]什么是 kambo 毒药？Kambo 被认为是有毒的，因为它含有生物活性肽（短链氨基酸）的复杂混合物，其中一些会猛烈刺激消化道，引起强烈的恶心和呕吐，而另一些则直接作用于心血管系统，导致血压迅速下降和昏厥。有些肽具有阿片类药物的成分影响，改变呼吸模式并影响中枢神经系统。在极端情况下，这种组合可能会引发突发的心脏问题或严重的器官毒性，这可能是致命的。在仪式之前，通常建议接受 kambo 的人喝大量的水。再加上严重呕吐和液体转移，可能会导致低钠血症——血液中钠含量极低，有时被称为水中毒。严重的低钠血症会导致脑肿胀、癫痫发作、昏迷，最严重的情况下会导致脑死亡。Kambo 还会导致胃肠道剧烈收缩，从而导致食道破裂，从而导致死亡。Kambo 仪式的用途是什么？Kambo 被称为“净化”仪式，被认为可以为身体排毒，在某些传统中，可以增强生育能力、男子气概，甚至带来好运。它还用于治疗疾病和失调，包括成瘾、据美国健康信息网站 Healthline 报道，阿兹海默症、焦虑症、癌症、慢性疼痛、抑郁症、糖尿病、肝炎、艾滋病毒和艾滋病、感染、风湿病和血管疾病。但医生们表示，没有科学证据表明坎博能有效治疗这些疾病，现代医学病例报告反而将坎博与严重疾病和死亡联系起来。健康线列出了坎博可能产生的副作用，包括恶心、呕吐、腹泻、腹痛、头晕、心悸、心慌等。喉咙里有肿块，吞咽困难，嘴唇、眼睑或脸部肿胀，甚至膀胱失控。一名男子为坎博仪式准备青蛙毒药 [Shutterstock]谁在使用它？过去二十年来，坎博在西方和拉丁美洲国家流行起来。它已被巴西、澳大利亚、英国和美国的“健康”圈和替代医学社区采用。然而，土著专家警告非土著从业者不要使用卡姆博。英国媒体最近报道称，山绳党领导人若阿金·卢兹批评了网上销售坎博的行为，并警告称，未经原住民社区准备或许可而使用坎博会使人们面临风险。山那瓦人起源于巴西亚马逊的阿卡州，社区延伸至秘鲁和玻利维亚。谁死于卡姆博？近年来，全球范围内已知至少有六人因使用卡姆博而死亡。2008年，巴西一名52岁男子在接受卡姆博治疗后死亡。2018年，意大利一名男子在接受卡姆博治疗时因心率异常而死亡。该男子患有肥胖症和心室肥大，这种情况会导致心脏的主要泵室增厚。2019 年，39 岁的澳大利亚妇女 Natasha Lechner 在参加 kambo 仪式以治疗慢性背痛时死亡。2021 年 10 月，澳大利亚男子 Jarrad Antonovich 在接受 kambo 仪式后死亡。据怀疑他死于食道穿孔

contentParagraphs.en: Anthropic, the AI firm behind the Claude chatbot, announced on Thursday it had raised $65bn in funding to value the company at $965bn post-money. The move makes Anthropic the world’s most valuable AI startup, eclipsing its competitor OpenAI.The deal marks an exceedingly successful period of growth for Anthropic, which was once considered to be a smaller player in the global AI arms race. The widespread adoption of its products by large enterprise businesses, especially following its release of powerful coding assistants late last year, has turned it into a dominant player in the industry.Anthropic’s new valuation cements a reshuffling of the AI industry’s power dynamics, putting a dollar figure on Claude’s increased cultural and commercial prominence. The deal is also likely to have implications for this year’s blockbuster slate of initial public offerings, which includes rivals OpenAI and SpaceX.The company touted the new funding round, which was led by Altimeter Capital, Dragoneer, Greenoaks, and Sequoia Capital, as proof that its AI models and vision for the future of the technology are winning out.“Claude is increasingly indispensable to our growing global community of customers, and we work tirelessly to make tools like Claude Code and Cowork more helpful, more powerful, and more adaptable to their needs,” Krishna Rao, chief financial officer of Anthropic, said in a blogpost.In addition to orienting its business more towards enterprise and coding services than some of its consumer-forward competitors, Anthropic has also postured itself as a more safety-focused company. One of Anthropic’s co-founders was present earlier this month at Pope Leo’s release of a more than 43,000-word encyclical which warned against the dangers of AI and called for a reining-in of the technology.The firm is also still locked in a legal battle with the Pentagon following its refusal earlier this year to remove safeguards that would allow Claude to be used for mass domestic surveillance or lethal autonomous weapons systems, which could kill people without human input. The US government has reportedly used Claude for military operations including missile strikes on Iran and the capture of Nicolás Maduro, the Venezuelan leader.The White House was forced to ease its feud with Anthropic somewhat in recent weeks, however, after the company announced that it was withholding the release of its latest Mythos model over cybersecurity concerns. The episode sparked a small-scale geopolitical crisis as nations worried about vulnerabilities to financial systems and critical infrastructure, as well as prompting a call from JD Vance, the US vice-president, to the heads of AI companies urging their cooperation.Anthropic is additionally set to be an influential force in the US midterm elections, pouring millions into lobbying efforts and Super Pacs aimed at candidates and legislation that aligns with its views on regulating AI. The firm has called for more government oversight of the technology, breaking with other tech industry leaders and OpenAI which advocate for a more lax regulatory framework.The company’s valuation underscores the enormous amounts of money still flowing into the AI industry, despite widespread public distrust of the technology. Anthropic’s valuation follows OpenAI raising $122bn in March to be valued at $852bn, with the possibility it will seek a $1tn IPO later this year.
contentParagraphs.zh: Claude 聊天机器人背后的人工智能公司 Anthropic 周四宣布，已筹集 650 亿美元资金，使该公司的投后估值达到 9650 亿美元。此举使 Anthropic 成为全球最有价值的人工智能初创公司，超越了其竞争对手 OpenAI。这笔交易标志着 Anthropic 的一个极其成功的增长期，该公司曾被认为是全球人工智能军备竞赛中较小的参与者。其产品被大型企业广泛采用，特别是在去年年底发布了强大的编码助手之后，它已成为该行业的主导者。Anthropic 的新估值巩固了人工智能行业权力动态的重新洗牌，为 Claude 日益增强的文化和商业影响力奠定了基础。这笔交易还可能对今年的重磅首次公开募股产生影响，其中包括竞争对手 OpenAI 和 SpaceX。该公司宣传了由 Altimeter Capital、Dragoneer、Greenoaks 和红杉资本领投的新一轮融资，证明其人工智能模型和对技术未来的愿景正在获胜。 Anthropic 首席财务官 Krishna Rao 在一篇博文中表示：“我们的产品更有帮助、更强大、更能适应他们的需求。”Anthropic 的首席财务官 Krishna Rao 在一篇博文中表示。 除了比一些面向消费者的竞争对手更注重企业和编码服务之外，Anthropic 还将自己定位为一家更加注重安全的公司。 Anthropic 的一位联合创始人本月早些时候出席了教皇利奥 (Pope Leo) 发布的一份超过 43,000 字的通谕，该通谕警告人们警惕人工智能的危险，并呼吁控制该技术。该公司还与五角大楼陷入了一场法律战，因为五角大楼今年早些时候拒绝取消允许 Claude 用于大规模家庭监控或致命自主武器系统的保障措施，这些系统无需人工干预即可杀人。据报道，美国政府曾利用克劳德进行军事行动，包括对伊朗进行导弹袭击和抓捕委内瑞拉领导人尼古拉斯·马杜罗。然而，最近几周，在 Anthropic 公司宣布因网络安全问题而暂缓发布其最新的 Mythos 模型后，白宫被迫在一定程度上缓和了与 Anthropic 的不和。这一事件引发了一场小规模的地缘政治危机，各国担心金融系统和关键基础设施的脆弱性，并促使美国副总统 JD Vance 致电人工智能公司负责人，敦促他们合作。Anthropic 还将成为美国中期选举中的一支有影响力的力量，投入数百万美元用于针对候选人的游说活动和超级政治行动委员会，以及与其监管人工智能观点一致的立法。该公司呼吁政府加强对该技术的监管，与其他科技行业领导者和 OpenAI 主张更宽松的监管框架不同。该公司的估值突显出，尽管公众对该技术普遍不信任，但仍有大量资金流入人工智能行业。 Anthropic 的估值是在 OpenAI 三月份筹集 1220 亿美元后估值达到 8520 亿美元，并有可能在今年晚些时候寻求 1 万亿美元的 IPO 时实现的。

articles.title: FIFA's World Cup ticket sales outraged fans. Now they are under investigation
articles.summary: FIFA has always had a peculiar way to sell tickets to the World Cup. It never faced any major issues — until prices soared for the 2026 tournament.
articles.titleZh: 国际足联世界杯门票销售激怒了球迷。现在他们正在接受调查
articles.summaryZh: 国际足联一直有一种独特的方式来销售世界杯门票。它从未遇到过任何重大问题——直到 2026 年锦标赛的价格飙升。

contentParagraphs.en: FIFA — led by Gianni Infantino — is facing a joint probe from attorneys general in New Jersey and New York.
contentParagraphs.zh: 由詹尼·因凡蒂诺领导的国际足联正面临新泽西州和纽约州总检察长的联合调查。

contentParagraphs.en: Win McNamee/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 赢得麦克纳米/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: Win McNamee/Getty Images
contentParagraphs.zh: 赢得麦克纳米/盖蒂图片社

contentParagraphs.en: FIFA President Gianni Infantino has had an answer when asked about the sky-high ticket prices for this summer's World Cup: They are simply adapting to the North American market.
contentParagraphs.zh: 当被问及今夏世界杯天价票价时，国际足联主席因凡蒂诺给出了这样的答案：他们只是在适应北美市场。

contentParagraphs.en: That's the justification FIFA has used to sharply increase ticket prices for the World Cup taking place in the United States, Mexico and Canada this summer compared to previous tournaments.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这就是国际足联大幅提高今年夏天在美国、墨西哥和加拿大举行的世界杯门票价格的理由。

contentParagraphs.en: But those increases could come with some serious consequences: The New York and New Jersey attorneys general said on Wednesday they have launched a probe into FIFA over ticket practices.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但这些增加可能会带来一些严重的后果：纽约和新泽西州总检察长周三表示，他们已对国际足联的门票做法展开调查。

contentParagraphs.en: And it's not just the ticket prices. New York Atty Gen. Letitia James and New Jersey Atty Gen. Jennifer Davenport are also looking at FIFA's sales process, including the way it has allocated tickets to fans and whether the organization's sales tactics have contributed to "soaring prices."
contentParagraphs.zh: 这不仅仅是票价。纽约州检察长利蒂西亚·詹姆斯和新泽西州检察长詹妮弗·达文波特也在关注国际足联的销售流程，包括向球迷分配门票的方式以及该组织的销售策略是否导致了“价格飙升”。

contentParagraphs.en: Here's what to know about how FIFA sells tickets — and why it has become such a flash point with just two weeks to go before the start of the tournament.
contentParagraphs.zh: 以下是关于国际足联如何出售门票的信息，以及为什么在距离世界杯开始仅剩两周的时间里它成为了一个热点。

contentParagraphs.en: FIFA's prices draw condemnation
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际足联的价格招致谴责

contentParagraphs.en: Ticket prices for this tournament aren't just much higher than previous World Cups. For the first time, FIFA has introduced dynamic pricing, leading to sharply higher prices for many World Cup games.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本届世界杯的门票价格不仅比往届世界杯高出很多。国际足联首次引入动态定价，导致许多世界杯比赛的价格大幅上涨。

contentParagraphs.en: A clear example is for the final match, set to take place in New Jersey on July 19. FIFA initially sold the most expensive tickets at $6,730 — already much higher than the about $1,600 price for the most expensive tickets for the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar.
contentParagraphs.zh: 一个明显的例子是定于 7 月 19 日在新泽西州举行的决赛。国际足联最初以 6,730 美元的价格出售最贵的门票，这已经远远高于 2022 年卡塔尔国际足联世界杯最贵门票的约 1,600 美元。

contentParagraphs.en: By its latest sales windows starting in April, the same category of tickets cost $10,990.
contentParagraphs.zh: 截至 4 月份开始的最新销售窗口，同类门票售价为 10,990 美元。

contentParagraphs.en: The prices have sparked widespread outrage — and drawn wide scrutiny on FIFA. Even President Trump, who has established a close relationship with Infantino, told the New York Post he wouldn't pay the roughly $1,000 for nosebleed seats for the U.S. opening game against Paraguay next month.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些价格引发了广泛的愤怒，并引起了国际足联的广泛审查。就连与因凡蒂诺关系密切的特朗普总统也对《纽约邮报》表示，他不会为下个月美国队对阵巴拉圭队的揭幕战支付大约 1,000 美元的流鼻血座位费。

contentParagraphs.en: Compounding the problem, FIFA back in April also unveiled an entirely new category of tickets called "Front Category" seats, offering front row seats across the stadium, that were priced even more expensively.
contentParagraphs.zh: 使问题更加复杂的是，国际足联在四月份还推出了一种全新的门票类别，称为“前类别”座位，提供整个体育场的前排座位，但价格更加昂贵。

contentParagraphs.en: A front row seat in the lower bowl for the final costs more than $30,000.
contentParagraphs.zh: 决赛下层碗区的前排座位售价超过 30,000 美元。

contentParagraphs.en: FIFA's sales tactics also under scrutiny
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际足联的销售策略也受到审查

contentParagraphs.en: One looming legal question is whether FIFA acted as a monopoly and unfairly used its status to sell tickets, attorneys and ticketing experts told NPR.
contentParagraphs.zh: 律师和票务专家告诉美国国家公共广播电台，一个迫在眉睫的法律问题是，国际足联是否充当垄断者，不公平地利用其地位来出售门票。

contentParagraphs.en: Derek Howard, an attorney who also teaches at the University of San Francisco, said FIFA has closely guarded information about how it's pricing tickets, how many tickets are left to sell and when it decides to adjust prices. That's kept ticket buyers in the dark, and FIFA holding all the cards.
contentParagraphs.zh: 同时在旧金山大学任教的律师德里克·霍华德表示，国际足联一直严格保密有关门票定价方式、剩余门票数量以及何时决定调整价格的信息。这让购票者蒙在鼓里，而国际足联掌握着所有的牌。

contentParagraphs.en: "FIFA has been using what we might call scare tactics to create a demand for a ticket and telling people that they have to pay the high prices because if they don't, they're going to miss out on this once in a lifetime event," Howard says. "The problem behind that is that the consumers have no way of knowing whether or not that's true."
contentParagraphs.zh: 霍华德说：“国际足联一直在使用我们所谓的恐吓策略来创造门票需求，并告诉人们他们必须支付高价，因为如果他们不这样做，他们就会错过这场一生一次的赛事。” “这背后的问题是消费者无法知道这是否属实。”

contentParagraphs.en: Infantino poses with President Trump, Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum, and Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney at the World Cup draw ceremony held in Washington, D.C., on Dec. 5, 2025.
contentParagraphs.zh: 因凡蒂诺于 2025 年 12 月 5 日在华盛顿特区举行的世界杯抽签仪式上与特朗普总统、墨西哥总统克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆和加拿大总理马克·卡尼合影。

contentParagraphs.en: Even in the current "last-minute sales" window, FIFA releases tickets across games at periodic times — while encouraging fans to act now. That means someone logging in any given day has no way to know if the tickets being offered are the last remaining or whether more will be available later and at what price.
contentParagraphs.zh: 即使在当前的“最后一刻销售”窗口中，国际足联也会定期在比赛中发布门票，同时鼓励球迷立即采取行动。这意味着登录任何一天的用户都无法知道所提供的门票是否是最后一张门票，或者以后是否会有更多门票以什么价格出售。

contentParagraphs.en: "Being honest about ticket sales is not complicated. But FIFA has turned buying a ticket to the World Cup into a gauntlet of confusion, fake scarcity, and impossibly high prices — all at the expense of consumers and hardworking New Jerseyans," Davenport said in her statement.
contentParagraphs.zh: “对门票销售诚实并不复杂。但国际足联已经把购买世界杯门票变成了一场混乱、虚假稀缺和不可能的高价格的挑战--所有这些都是以牺牲消费者和勤奋的新泽西人为代价的。”达文波特在她的声明中说。

contentParagraphs.en: FIFA has wide discretion in assigning seat locations
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际足联在分配座位位置方面拥有广泛的自由裁量权

contentParagraphs.en: One of the big peculiarities of World Cup ticket sales is that fans are not able to pick a specific seat until much later in the process.
contentParagraphs.zh: 世界杯门票销售的一大特点是，球迷要到很久以后才能选择特定的座位。

contentParagraphs.en: Initially, fans can only pick between four broad ticket categories; later they are assigned specific seats by FIFA. That's not typical in the U.S., where fans are accustomed to picking the seat they want at the time of purchase.
contentParagraphs.zh: 最初，球迷只能在四大门票类别中进行选择；后来，国际足联为他们分配了特定的座位。这在美国并不常见，在美国，粉丝习惯于在购买时选择自己想要的座位。

contentParagraphs.en: Seating maps for most stadiums like the one in New Jersey indicated that Category 1 tickets — or the most expensive ones — cover large parts of the lower bowl of a stadium. By glancing at those stadium maps, fans could assume they had a good shot at getting a prime sideline view. What FIFA doesn't clearly spell out is that a number of those sideline seats are reserved for premium tickets and other categories of ticket holders such as corporate sponsors.
contentParagraphs.zh: 大多数体育场（如新泽西州的体育场）的座位地图显示， 1类门票（或最昂贵的门票）覆盖了体育场下碗的大部分区域。通过浏览这些体育场地图，球迷们可能会认为他们有一个很好的机会获得一个主要的边线视图。国际足联没有明确说明的是，其中一些副业座位是为高级门票和其他类别的门票持有者（如企业赞助商）保留的。

contentParagraphs.en: There's no way of knowing how many seats are left in any of the sections — and many fans who bought those expensive Category 1 tickets have complained they got stuck in less desirable seats, such as behind corners.
contentParagraphs.zh: 没有办法知道任何部分还有多少座位—许多购买这些昂贵的第一类门票的粉丝抱怨他们被困在不太理想的座位上，例如角落后面。

contentParagraphs.en: This World Cup is not the first time FIFA has used this blind ticketing system, but it is the first time it's charged such high prices, sparking outrage among fans who feel they've been overcharged.
contentParagraphs.zh: 本届世界杯并不是国际足联第一次使用这种盲目售票系统，但这是第一次收取如此高的价格，引发了觉得自己被收费过高的球迷的愤怒。

contentParagraphs.en: FIFA can also change seating charts
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际足联也可以更改座位表

contentParagraphs.en: Like most sports, FIFA divvies up the stadium seating map into different categories with different prices. Lower sideline seats are more expensive than those in the high up, "nosebleed section," for example.
contentParagraphs.zh: 与大多数体育项目一样，国际足联将体育场座位地图划分为不同的类别，价格也不同。例如，较低的侧排座椅比较高的侧排座椅更昂贵，例如“流鼻血部分”。

contentParagraphs.en: But FIFA reserves the right to adjust seating charts as it wants. That means, FIFA could increase the number of seats considered Category 1, so a fan who thought they were buying in a top-tier section could end up in a previously lower-category seat.
contentParagraphs.zh: 但国际足联保留根据需要调整座位表的权利。这意味着，国际足联可以增加被视为第1类的座位数量，因此，认为自己在顶级区域购买的球迷最终可能会获得以前较低类别的座位。

contentParagraphs.en: FIFA has traditionally sold tickets this way. But James took issue with that.
contentParagraphs.zh: 国际足联传统上以这种方式出售门票。但詹姆斯对此表示不满。

contentParagraphs.en: "No one should be manipulated into payin
contentParagraphs.zh: "任何人都不应被操纵进入付费系统

articles.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on the launch pad during an engine-firing test
articles.summary: A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week.
articles.titleZh: 蓝色起源火箭在发动机点火测试期间在发射台上爆炸
articles.summaryZh: 周四晚上，杰夫·贝佐斯的蓝色起源公司的一枚火箭在发射台进行的一次测试中爆炸，该火箭计划于下周发射卫星。

contentParagraphs.en: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket stands ready for launch at the Cape Canaveral Space Force station in Cape Canaveral, Fla., April 18, 2026.
contentParagraphs.zh: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket stands ready for launch at the Cape Canaveral Space Force station in Cape Canaveral, Fla., April 18, 2026.

contentParagraphs.en: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. — A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, shaking nearby homes and briefly painting the sky orange.
contentParagraphs.zh: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. — A rocket belonging to Jeff Bezos' Blue Origin exploded during a test at the launch pad Thursday night, shaking nearby homes and briefly painting the sky orange.

contentParagraphs.en: Blue Origin said its New Glenn rocket exploded during an engine-firing test being conducted ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week. No one was hurt, according to officials at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.
contentParagraphs.zh: Blue Origin said its New Glenn rocket exploded during an engine-firing test being conducted ahead of a satellite launch planned for next week. No one was hurt, according to officials at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station.

contentParagraphs.en: "It's too early to know the root cause but we're already working to find it," Bezos said via X. "Very rough day, but we'll rebuild whatever needs rebuilding and get back to flying. It's worth it."
contentParagraphs.zh: "It's too early to know the root cause but we're already working to find it," Bezos said via X. "Very rough day, but we'll rebuild whatever needs rebuilding and get back to flying. It's worth it."

contentParagraphs.en: The massive New Glenn was grounded in April after it left a satellite in the wrong orbit because of engine failure. It was only the third flight of the rocket that Blue Origin intends to use to launch landers to the moon for NASA, including the landers that will take astronauts to the lunar surface.
contentParagraphs.zh: The massive New Glenn was grounded in April after it left a satellite in the wrong orbit because of engine failure. It was only the third flight of the rocket that Blue Origin intends to use to launch landers to the moon for NASA, including the landers that will take astronauts to the lunar surface.

contentParagraphs.en: The company had been on track to launch a prototype lunar lander to the moon on a flight test this fall. Earlier this week, the space agency awarded Blue Origin a contract worth hundreds of millions of dollars to launch a pair of moon buggies in the next few years as part of the Artemis program.
contentParagraphs.zh: The company had been on track to launch a prototype lunar lander to the moon on a flight test this fall. Earlier this week, the space agency awarded Blue Origin a contract worth hundreds of millions of dollars to launch a pair of moon buggies in the next few years as part of the Artemis program.

contentParagraphs.en: "Spaceflight is unforgiving, and developing new heavy-lift launch capability is extraordinarily difficult," NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said via X. He promised to provide information on any impacts to the Artemis program, including the moon base that he recently outlined.
contentParagraphs.zh: "Spaceflight is unforgiving, and developing new heavy-lift launch capability is extraordinarily difficult," NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman said via X. He promised to provide information on any impacts to the Artemis program, including the moon base that he recently outlined.

contentParagraphs.en: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes during an engine-firing test on Thursday, May 28, 2026, in Cape Canaveral, Fla.
contentParagraphs.zh: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket explodes during an engine-firing test on Thursday, May 28, 2026, in Cape Canaveral, Fla.

contentParagraphs.en: Homes shook in nearby Cape Canaveral and Cocoa Beach around 9 p.m., with residents turning to social media to wonder what happened. Launch Complex 36 is visible from the beach, and the internet quickly filled with photos of an orange fireball.
contentParagraphs.zh: Homes shook in nearby Cape Canaveral and Cocoa Beach around 9 p.m., with residents turning to social media to wonder what happened. Launch Complex 36 is visible from the beach, and the internet quickly filled with photos of an orange fireball.

contentParagraphs.en: The rocket was supposed to blast off next week with internet satellites that are part of the Amazon Leo constellation in orbit.
contentParagraphs.zh: The rocket was supposed to blast off next week with internet satellites that are part of the Amazon Leo constellation in orbit.

contentParagraphs.en: Emergency crews remained more than an hour after the explosion. Officials stressed there was no threat due to fumes or other potential hazards.
contentParagraphs.zh: Emergency crews remained more than an hour after the explosion. Officials stressed there was no threat due to fumes or other potential hazards.

contentParagraphs.en: Space Force officials said the explosion would not affect upcoming launches by other companies from other pads. United Launch Alliance's Atlas V rocket is due to blast off Friday night with a batch of Amazon Leo satellites, the same kind that this rocket was supposed to take up.
contentParagraphs.zh: Space Force officials said the explosion would not affect upcoming launches by other companies from other pads. United Launch Alliance's Atlas V rocket is due to blast off Friday night with a batch of Amazon Leo satellites, the same kind that this rocket was supposed to take up.

contentParagraphs.en: SpaceX's Elon Musk, who's had his own share of rocket explosions, offered his condolences. "Sorry to see this, I hope you recover quickly," he told Blue Origin via X.
contentParagraphs.zh: SpaceX's Elon Musk, who's had his own share of rocket explosions, offered his condolences. "Sorry to see this, I hope you recover quickly," he told Blue Origin via X.

contentParagraphs.en: Towering at 321 feet (98 meters), New Glenn made its debut in 2025. It is named after John Glenn, the first American to orbit Earth, and is much bigger and more powerful than the New Shepard rockets that have carried tourists to the fringes of space from Texas.
contentParagraphs.zh: Towering at 321 feet (98 meters), New Glenn made its debut in 2025. It is named after John Glenn, the first American to orbit Earth, and is much bigger and more powerful than the New Shepard rockets that have carried tourists to the fringes of space from Texas.

articles.title: Sudan medical group says RSF-affiliated fighters kill 27 civilians
articles.summary: The attacks come as nearly 19.5 million Sudanese face severe hunger amid the country’s deepening humanitarian crisis.
articles.titleZh: 苏丹医疗组织称无国界医生组织附属武装分子杀死 27 名平民
articles.summaryZh: 这些袭击发生之际，苏丹人道主义危机日益严重，近 1950 万苏丹人面临严重饥饿。

contentParagraphs.en: The attacks come as nearly 19.5 million Sudanese face severe hunger amid the country’s deepening humanitarian crisis.A force affiliated with the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) has killed at least 27 people, including elderly residents, in an attack on villages west of Bara in Sudan’s North Kordofan state, according to the Sudan Doctors Network.The Cairo-based medical NGO said the attacks took place on Thursday in the al-Murrah area, describing them as “a new crime targeting unarmed civilians in areas with no military presence”.Recommended Stories list of 4 itemslist 1 of 4French prosecutors to probe Israeli treatment of flotilla activistslist 2 of 4‘Brazilians of Europe’: How France created football’s deepest talent poollist 3 of 4UN urges ‘urgent’ action to protect children onlinelist 4 of 4Firefighters work to free riders stuck atop a rollercoaster in Texasend of listSudan has been engulfed in civil war since April 2023, when long-running tensions between the Sudanese army and the RSF erupted into a full-scale conflict that has killed hundreds of thousands of people and displaced millions.The Kordofan region has become one of the war’s main battlegrounds, with fighting intensifying across several fronts, including through drone attacks.The RSF and allied groups control much of the western Darfur region, as well as parts of Kordofan along the border with South Sudan, areas rich in oil fields and gold mines. The paramilitary force has also repeatedly clashed with the army over the town of Bara.Thursday’s assault took place during the second day of Eid al-Adha.In a statement posted on Facebook, the Sudan Doctors Network said that “targeting villages and civilian areas and executing citizens in such a brutal manner constitutes a flagrant violation of international humanitarian law and all norms and conventions that prohibit attacks on civilians, especially amid the catastrophic humanitarian conditions people are enduring because of the ongoing war”.The group added that the “continued attacks on civilians and safe villages” are worsening the humanitarian crisis and forcing more families into displacement, suffering and the loss of their livelihoods.The attacks come as more than 40 percent of Sudan’s population faces acute hunger, according to a report released on Thursday by the United Nations-backed Integrated Food Security Phase Classification (IPC).The report said nearly 19.5 million people across the country are facing severe food insecurity as the conflict drives what aid agencies describe as one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises.The Sudan Doctors Network also called on the “international community and human rights and humanitarian organizations to condemn these violations and act urgently to protect civilians and stop the repeated attacks on residential areas by pressuring RSF leaders to end violations against civilians”.
contentParagraphs.zh: 这些袭击发生之际，该国人道主义危机日益严重，近 1950 万苏丹人面临严重饥饿。据苏丹医生网络报道，准军事快速支援部队 (RSF) 下属的一支部队在苏丹北科尔多凡州巴拉以西村庄的袭击中，造成至少 27 人死亡，其中包括老年居民。总部位于开罗的医疗非政府组织表示，袭击发生在周四的穆拉地区，并将其描述为“针对目标群体的新犯罪”。推荐故事列表 4 项列表 4 项中的 1 项法国检察官调查以色列对待船队积极分子的方式列表 4 项中的 2 项“欧洲巴西人”：法国如何创建足球界最深厚的人才库列表 4 项中的 3 项联合国敦促采取“紧急”行动保护在线儿童列表 4 项中的 4 项消防队员为被困在德克萨斯州过山车上的搭便车者提供帮助 列表结束自 2023 年 4 月以来，苏丹军队与无国界部队之间的长期紧张关系爆发为全面冲突，导致数十万人死亡，数百万人流离失所。科尔多凡地区已成为战争的主战场之一，多条战线的战斗不断加剧，包括无人机袭击。无国界医生组织和盟军控制着达尔富尔西部地区的大部分地区，以及与南苏丹接壤的科尔多凡地区的部分地区。拥有丰富的油田和金矿。准军事部队还多次与军队就巴拉镇发生冲突。周四的袭击发生在古尔邦节第二天。苏丹医生网络在 Facebook 上发布的一份声明中表示，“以村庄和平民地区为目标，以如此残忍的方式处决公民，公然违反了国际人道法以及禁止攻击平民的所有规范和公约，特别是在人们因持续战争而遭受灾难性人道主义状况的情况下”。该组织补充说，“对平民和安全村庄的持续袭击”正在加剧人道主义危机，迫使更多家庭流离失所、遭受苦难和失去生计。根据联合国支持的综合粮食安全阶段分类（IPC）周四发布的一份报告，袭击发生之际，苏丹超过 40% 的人口面临严重饥饿。报告称，由于冲突推动了援助机构所描述的世界上最严重的粮食不安全之一，该国近 1950 万人面临严重的粮食不安全。苏丹医生网络还呼吁“国际社会以及人权和人道主义组织谴责这些侵犯行为，并采取紧急行动保护平民，并通过向无国界医生组织领导人施压，要求其停止针对平民的侵犯行为，制止对居民区的反复袭击”。

articles.title: California Attorney General sues 23andMe successor for 2023 data breach
articles.summary: Attorney General Rob Bonta alleges the company lied about the breach's severity.
articles.titleZh: 加州总检察长因 2023 年数据泄露起诉 23andMe 继任者
articles.summaryZh: 司法部长罗布·邦塔 (Rob Bonta) 指控该公司在泄露事件的严重性方面撒了谎。

contentParagraphs.en: California Attorney General sues 23andMe successor for 2023 data breach21 hours agoLily JamaliNorth America Technology correspondentReutersCalifornia Attorney General Rob Bonta has said he will sue DNA testing firm Chrome Holding following a probe on Thursday, alleging its predecessor company 23andMe failed to protect sensitive customer data.Bonta said the failure resulted in a 2023 data breach which exposed genetic predispositions and risk factors of nearly seven million users, plus information about biological relatives, ancestry, and ethnicity."Our investigation found that the company failed to take basic steps to protect users' data," said Bonta, who added 23andMe "lied to consumers about the severity of its 2023 data breach."The BBC has requested comment from Chrome Holding. The company was rebranded after 23andMe filed for bankruptcy last year.Bonta also alleges the subsequent sale of 23andMe user data on the dark web by threat actors specifically touted that it belonged to Asian American Pacific Islanders (AAPI) and Jewish users."This is disturbing and incredibly dangerous" given it occurred during a period of "mounting anti-Asian American and Pacific Islander and antisemitic hate and violence," Bonta said.Users were targeted by a so-called "credential stuffing" attack in which hackers used passwords exposed in previous breaches to access 23andMe accounts for which people had used similar credentials.The 2023 data breach has resulted in international regulatory scrutiny for the company.The ICO said personal data of 155,592 UK residents was accessed.The company has said it has "made several binding commitments to enhance protections for customer data and privacy."The ICO's probe was conducted in coordination with Canada's privacy commissioner and found 23andMe violated UK law by failing to implement appropriate authentication and verification measures for customers during its login process.At the time, some users expressed concern over the prospect of insurance companies purchasing their data and using it to determine whether to provide coverage.23andMe was cofounded by Anne Wojcicki, sister of the late YouTube boss Susan Wojcicki and ex-wife of Google co-founder Sergey Brin.The company once counted Snoop Dogg, Oprah Winfrey, and Eva Longoria as customers and saw its share price top $300 at its peak before crashing in 2024.
contentParagraphs.zh: 加州总检察长起诉 23andMe 继任者 2023 年数据泄露 21 小时前 莉莉·贾玛利 (Lily Jamali) 北美科技通讯员路透社 加州总检察长罗布·邦塔 (Rob Bonta) 表示，他将在周四进行调查后起诉 DNA 检测公司 Chrome Holding，指控其前身公司 23andMe 未能保护敏感客户数据。Bonta 表示，这一失败导致了 2023 年数据泄露，暴露了近 700 万用户的遗传倾向和风险因素， Bonta 表示：“我们的调查发现，该公司未能采取基本措施来保护用户数据。”他补充道，23andMe“就 2023 年数据泄露的严重性向消费者撒了谎。”BBC 已要求 Chrome Holding 发表评论。去年 23andMe 申请破产后，该公司被重新命名。Bonta 还声称，威胁行为者随后在暗网上出售了 23andMe 用户数据，并专门宣称这些数据属于亚裔美国人太平洋岛民 (AAPI) 和犹太用户。邦塔说，鉴于它发生在“反亚裔美国人和太平洋岛民以及反犹太仇恨和暴力日益严重”的时期，“这是令人不安和极其危险的”。 “撞库”攻击，黑客使用之前泄露的密码访问人们使用类似凭据的 23andMe 帐户。2023 年的数据泄露导致该公司受到国际监管审查。ICO 表示，155,592 名英国居民的个人数据被访问。该公司表示，它“做出了几项具有约束力的承诺，以加强对客户数据和隐私的保护。”ICO 的调查是与加拿大隐私专员协调进行的并发现 23andMe 在登录过程中未能对客户实施适当的身份验证和验证措施，违反了英国法律。当时，一些用户对保险公司购买其数据并利用这些数据来确定是否提供承保的前景表示担忧。23andMe 是由已故 YouTube 老板苏珊·沃西基 (Susan Wojcicki) 的妹妹、谷歌联合创始人谢尔盖·布林 (Sergey Brin) 的前妻安妮·沃西基 (Anne Wojcicki) 共同创立的。该公司曾数过史努比狗狗 (Snoop Dogg)、奥普拉·温弗瑞 (Oprah Winfrey) 和伊娃 (Eva)。 Longoria 是其客户，其股价在 2024 年暴跌之前曾一度突破 300 美元。

articles.title: Moment Blue Origin rocket explodes during test in Florida
articles.summary: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on a launch pad at Cape Canaveral in Florida on Thursday night.
articles.titleZh: 蓝色起源火箭在佛罗里达州测试期间爆炸的瞬间
articles.summaryZh: 周四晚上，蓝色起源公司的一枚新格伦火箭在佛罗里达州卡纳维拉尔角的发射台上爆炸。

contentParagraphs.en: A Blue Origin New Glenn rocket exploded on a launch pad at Cape Canaveral in Florida on Thursday night.The space technology company, which was founded in 2000 by Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos, said there were no injuries from the incident. "All personnel have been accounted for. We will provide updates as we learn more," Blue Origin said in a statement. The explosion occurred during a "hotfire test", the company said, describing the incident as an "anomaly". Read more on this story here.

articles.title: QUAD to build Fiji port: A new US-China flashpoint?
articles.summary: Quad kicks back to life in Asia Pacific, looking to develop ports in Fiji, in a counter to China's rise.
articles.titleZh: 四方建设斐济港：美中新的爆发点？
articles.summaryZh: Quad在亚太地区重新焕发活力，寻求开发斐济的港口，以对抗中国的崛起。

contentParagraphs.en: New Delhi, India – The foreign ministers of India, the United States, Australia and Japan have announced plans to jointly develop port infrastructure in Fiji, a strategically important Pacific island nation, following their meeting in New Delhi earlier this week.Their grouping, called the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue alliance – or Quad, for short – has worked over the past two decades as a counter force to China’s economic and political influence in the Asia Pacific region.China, in turn, has said groupings should “not target any third party”, and Beijing opposes “engaging in bloc confrontation”.Australian Foreign Minister Penny Wong hailed the initiative in Fiji as the strongest commitment to deliver high-quality infrastructure that the Quad has made in the Pacific region. She said a pilot for port infrastructure in Fiji reflects the group’s ability to deliver high-quality infrastructure in response to Pacific priorities.As the grouping faces questions about its relevance in today’s world order, and US President Donald Trump has been wooing China, Washington has pivoted away from the region as its top priority in favour of the Western Hemisphere and the Middle East.So, what is the significance of a port development in Fiji? And could this be the next flashpoint in the up-and-down relationship between the US and China?Penny Wong, Australia’s foreign affairs minister, centre, Subrahmanyam Jaishankar, India’s external affairs minister, fourth left, and US Secretary of State Antony Blinken arrive for the Quadrilateral Security Dialogue (Quad) ministerial meeting on July 29, 2024 in Tokyo, Japan [Kiyoshi Ota/Getty Images]What are the plans for port development in Fiji?Fiji’s foreign minister said Suva and Lautoka would be the first ports to be developed on the island under a pilot project by the newly announced “Quad Ports of the Future Partnership”.This marks the first such joint initiative by the Quad.Suva, the capital, is already the largest and busiest port of the country. Lautoka, which was historically established around sugar exports, sits in the northwest of Fiji, in the second-largest city.“It will have to be the two major ones,” Sakiasi Ditoka told the Fijian press, referring to Suva and Lautoka. “And then eventually we’ll probably spread it around – maybe Vanua Levu if you look north, and then maybe Levuka if the project goes that far.”However, there are as yet very few specific details about plans for the ports.The Suva port agency, 41 percent of which is owned by Fiji’s government, has discussed $181m of port upgrades, as well as a potential $1.82bn plan to relocate Suva Port, with the US officials, Fijian media reported this week.Suresh Prasad, the Fiji Ports acting chief executive officer who said he was “rather surprised” by the announcement, told reporters: “If it’s a Quad project, it is going to be a mega project, which is Suva Port most likely.”In 2023, Fiji’s Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka discussed the redevelopment of the Suva port with China, but plans for that did not materialise.Rabuka told the Fijian parliament this Monday about an agreement to undertake a study for potential US-backed infrastructure with the US Millennium Challenge Corporation, Washington’s foreign aid agency, adding that any project would be funded by grants, not loans.What does the Quad do in the Asia Pacific?The four Quad countries are the most influential powers in the region outside of Russia and China.The Quad’s area of cooperation has expanded beyond its original security-focused outlook and now stretches to a wide range of Asia Pacific initiatives for maritime security, infrastructure, technology, health and supply chains.The grouping also operates the Indo-Pacific Partnership for Maritime Domain Awareness (IPMDA), which helps Pacific countries monitor illegal fishing, smuggling and other maritime activity with the use of satellite data. It cooperates on facilitating supply chains for lithium, rare-earth minerals and other strategic minerals to reduce dependence on China, alongside cooperation on quantum computing and cybersecurity.Fiji has traditionally been aligned with Australia, and the Quad countries have pledged cooperation on disaster relief, climate resilience and humanitarian assistance to smaller Pacific islands.Quad countries also hold joint military exercises under the annual Malabar Exercise in the region, including anti-submarine warfare, aircraft carrier operations and maritime interoperability.Chinese President Xi Jinping speaks during a meeting with Fiji’s Prime Minister Sitiveni Rabuka at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing, China, August 20, 2024 [Andres Martinez Casres/Reuters]Why is China unhappy about the Quad?“China perceives the Quad as an anti-China coalition, and this new project in Fiji is viewed as the Quad expanding its operations from Indo-Pacific hotspots into the Pacific Island countries,” said Sana Hashmi, a postdoctoral fellow at the Taiwan-Asia Exchange Foundation with a special focus on China.Over the past few years, Beijing has pumped billions of dollars into the region, and its influence has risen in parallel.In response to a question from the media this week about the Quad’s initiative in Fiji, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Mao Ning warned that any cooperation in the region “should not target any third party”.“China has stated its position on the Quad on multiple occasions. Cooperation between countries should be conducive to regional peace, stability, and prosperity, and not target any third party,” she told reporters. “We oppose forming exclusive groupings or engaging in bloc confrontation.”In a joint statement following their meeting in New Delhi this week, the Quad countries’ foreign ministers also expressed concern about rising tensions in the East China Sea and the South China Sea, reiterating “strong opposition to any destabilising or unilateral actions, including by force or coercion, that threaten peace and stability in the regi
contentParagraphs.zh: 印度新德里 — 印度、美国、澳大利亚和日本外长本周早些时候在新德里会晤后，宣布了在具有重要战略意义的太平洋岛国斐济联合开发港口基础设施的计划。他们的组织被称为四边安全对话联盟（简称四方），在过去二十年里一直致力于对抗中国在亚太地区的经济和政治影响力。中国则表示，该组织不应“针对任何第三方”，而北京反对“搞集团对抗”。澳大利亚外长黄英贤盛赞斐济的倡议是四方在太平洋地区做出的提供高质量基础设施的最有力承诺。她表示，斐济港口基础设施试点项目反映了该集团提供高质量基础设施以应对太平洋优先事项的能力。由于该集团面临着其在当今世界秩序中的相关性的质疑，加上美国总统特朗普一直在拉拢中国，华盛顿已将重心从该地区转向西半球和中东。那么，斐济港口开发的意义何在？这是否会成为中美关系上下波动的下一个爆发点？澳大利亚外交部长黄英贤（中）、印度外交部长苏杰生（左四）和美国国务卿安东尼·布林肯将于 2024 年 7 月 29 日抵达日本东京参加四边安全对话 (Quad) 部长级会议 [Kiyoshi Ota/Getty Images]斐济？斐济外交部长表示，苏瓦和劳托卡将成为新宣布的“未来伙伴关系四方港口”试点项目下首批在岛上开发的港口。这标志着四方港口首次开展此类联合倡议。首都苏瓦已经是该国最大、最繁忙的港口。劳托卡历史上是围绕食糖出口而建立的，位于斐济西北部，是第二大城市。“这必须是两个主要城市，”萨基西·迪托卡告诉斐济媒体，他指的是苏瓦和劳托卡。 “然后最终我们可能会将其扩展到各地——如果你向北看，可能是瓦努阿岛，如果项目进展到那么远，可能是莱武岛。”然而，关于港口计划的具体细节还很少。 据斐济媒体报道，苏瓦港口机构（其中斐济政府拥有 41% 的股份）已与美国官员讨论了价值 1.81 亿美元的港口升级，以及可能价值 18.2 亿美元的苏瓦港搬迁计划。斐济港口代理首席执行官苏雷什·普拉萨德 (Suresh Prasad) 表示，他对这一消息感到“相当惊讶”，他告诉记者：“如果这是一个 Quad 项目，那么这将是一个大型项目，最有可能的是苏瓦港。” 2023 年，斐济总理西蒂维尼·拉布卡 (Sitiveni Rabuka) 与中国讨论了苏瓦港的重建计划，但这一计划并未实现。拉布卡本周一向斐济议会表示，将签署一项开展研究的协议与华盛顿的对外援助机构美国千年挑战公司合作，寻求美国支持的潜在基础设施建设，并补充说，任何项目都将通过赠款而不是贷款来资助。四方在亚太地区做什么？四方四个国家是该地区除俄罗斯和中国之外最有影响力的国家。四方的合作领域已经超出了其最初以安全为重点的前景，现在已扩展到海事安全、基础设施、技术、卫生和供应链等广泛的亚太倡议。该组织还运营印度-太平洋海域意识伙伴关系（IPMDA），帮助太平洋国家利用卫星数据监控非法捕鱼、走私和其他海上活动。它在促进锂、稀土矿和其他战略矿产的供应链方面进行合作，以减少对中国的依赖，同时在量子计算和网络安全方面进行合作。斐济传统上与澳大利亚保持一致，四方国家已承诺在救灾、气候适应和向太平洋较小岛屿提供人道主义援助方面进行合作。四方国家还在该地区举行年度马拉巴尔演习期间举行联合军事演习，包括反潜战、航母行动和海上互操作性。中国国家主席习近平在与斐济总理会晤时发表讲话西蒂维尼·拉布卡部长于中国北京人民大会堂，2024 年 8 月 20 日 [安德烈斯·马丁内斯·卡斯雷斯/路透社]为什么中国对四方机制不满意？“中国认为四方机制是一个反华联盟，而斐济的这个新项目被视为四方机制将其业务从印度-太平洋热点地区扩展到太平洋岛国，”台湾亚洲交流基金会博士后研究员萨纳·哈什米（Sana Hashmi）说道。中国。过去几年，北京向该地区注入了数十亿美元，其影响力也随之上升。本周在回答媒体有关斐济四国倡议的问题时，中国外交部发言人毛宁警告称，该地区的任何合作“不应针对任何第三方”。她对记者说：“中方已多次在四方机制上阐明了立场。各国之间的合作应有利于地区和平、稳定与繁荣，而不是针对任何第三方。” “我们反对形成排他性集团或进行集团对抗。”本周在新德里举行会议后发表的联合声明中，四国外长还对东海和南海紧张局势升级表示担忧，重申“强烈反对任何威胁地区和平与稳定的破坏稳定或单边行动，包括武力或胁迫行动”。
